attacking_style: Pure sprinter who relies on positioning and lead-out trains rather than initiating attacks. Competes in flat/reduced bunch finishes, not an attacker in traditional sense.
team_tactics: UAE repeatedly subordinates his sprint ambitions to GC objectives. Team shows uncertainty in sprint leadership, sometimes bringing him as support rather than protected finisher. Lead-out execution inconsistent.
terrain_preference: Flat finishes and reduced bunch sprints. Exposed on any technical finales where positioning trumps raw speed. Not deployed for mountain stages except as domestique support.
vulnerability: Technical corners in sprint finales where experienced riders outposition him. Team hierarchy consistently relegates him when GC needs arise. Loses pure sprint leadership battles even within own team.
key_patterns: Sacrificed for GC riders multiple times; team hierarchy clearly favors stage racing over sprint opportunities (2019, 2022).
Positioning deficiencies in technical finishes; experienced riders take better wheels through corners (2021 Giro, Catalunya).
UAE sprint leadership ambiguity; brought as uncertain option or support rather than clear number one (2022, 2021).
Tactical role expanding beyond pure sprint/mountain support, contesting varied terrain by 2023 (Vuelta).
form_trajectory: neutral: expanded tactical role in 2023 suggests development, but no evidence of results improvement or clear leadership status
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 11
2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 4
